assertions.description: CustomElementRegistry now has a public constructor — `new CustomElementRegistry()` succeeds.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const r=new CustomElementRegistry();return r instanceof CustomElementRegistry;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: An instance of CustomElementRegistry exposes define() with the standard signature.
Element.attachShadow accepts a customElements option pointing at a scoped registry.
ShadowRoot exposes a customElements getter so script can introspect which registry is in effect.
Document.createElement accepts a customElementRegistry option so factory functions can use scoped registrations.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const reg=new CustomElementRegistry();class X extends HTMLElement{}reg.define('s-x',X);const e=document.createElement('s-x',{customElementRegistry:reg});return e instanceof X;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: The legacy global window.customElements registry remains for backwards compatibility.
